# Optional Integrations Checklist: Optional Setup Integrations Menu

**Purpose**: Validate requirement quality for the `./setup` optional integrations submenu, Codacy API management flow, and secret-safe agent discoverability before task generation.
**Created**: 2026-05-02
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all top-level project setup menu states covered for both empty and existing projects? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, FR-001, FR-002]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements complete for the optional integrations submenu entry, submenu choices, and cancel/back behavior? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, FR-003]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements complete enough to prevent Codacy from appearing as a top-level project setup item? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, SC-001]
- [x] CHK004 Are all core project setup actions named as protected from displacement by optional integrations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, SC-006]
- [x] CHK005 Are both Codacy credential modes fully specified with their required exposed variables? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, FR-006, FR-007]
- [x] CHK006 Are repository identity requirements complete for detected and user-provided owner/repository values? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Data Model §Project Repository Identity]
- [x] CHK007 Are requirements complete for token storage outside the project repository? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Contract §Codacy Environment Management]
- [x] CHK008 Are requirements complete for the project environment bridge across primary environment file, local bridge file, and token storage file? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Data Model §Project Environment Bridge]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK009 Is the term "optional integrations" sufficiently defined as non-critical project capabilities rather than required setup steps? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Data Model §Optional Integration]
- [x] CHK010 Is the boundary between top-level project setup and the optional integrations submenu unambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, FR-003, Contract §Project Setup Menu]
- [x] CHK011 Is "Manage Codacy API access" defined clearly enough to include repository-token and account-token setup but exclude unrelated Codacy workflow changes? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 2, FR-005, FR-006]
- [x] CHK012 Are the variable names and their purposes unambiguous for both Codacy credential modes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, FR-006, FR-007, Contract §Codacy Environment Management]
- [x] CHK013 Is "outside the project repository" specific enough to avoid ambiguity about where token values may be stored? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Data Model §Secret Storage Location]
- [x] CHK014 Is the required behavior for blank token input clearly specified for both existing and missing token files? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Secret Storage Location]
- [x] CHK015 Is the expected shell activation guidance clearly defined without requiring a specific shell implementation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015, Quickstart §Automated Validation]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK016 Do the spec, plan, and project setup menu contract use consistent wording for the optional submenu concept? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Summary, Contract §Project Setup Menu]
- [x] CHK017 Are repository-token requirements consistent between the spec, data model, and Codacy environment contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §Codacy Credential Mode, Contract §Repository Token Mode]
- [x] CHK018 Are account-token requirements consistent between the spec, data model, and Codacy environment contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §Codacy Credential Mode, Contract §Account Token Mode]
- [x] CHK019 Are secret-safety requirements consistent across generated agent guidance, project files, output, and test fixtures? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, FR-012, FR-014, SC-003]
- [x] CHK020 Are idempotency requirements consistent across the edge cases, data model, and Codacy contract? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Data Model §Project Environment Bridge, Contract §File Contract]
- [x] CHK021 Does the recommendation/highlight requirement align with the optional nature of integrations and avoid conflicting with future optional integration additions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK022 Are success criteria measurable for menu placement across empty and existing project sessions? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK023 Is the 3-minute completion criterion scoped clearly to users who already have a token? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK024 Is "0 token values appear" measurable across all named surfaces? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK025 Is the "exactly one managed Codacy environment section" outcome measurable after repeat setup? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK026 Is the cancellation success criterion measurable for both submenu cancellation and Codacy mode cancellation? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK027 Is the existing core-label stability criterion clear enough to evaluate after replacing the direct Codacy item? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-007]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK028 Are primary flows defined for entering project setup, opening optional integrations, and reaching Codacy management? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, User Story 2]
- [x] CHK029 Are alternate flows defined for repository-token and account-token Codacy modes? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK030 Are recovery flows defined for cancellation before a credential mode is completed? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, FR-013]
- [x] CHK031 Are repeat-run flows defined for updating an existing managed Codacy section without duplication? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, SC-004]
- [x] CHK032 Are flows defined for projects with and without detectable GitHub repository identity? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-008]
- [x] CHK033 Are flows defined for users who do not have direnv installed or active? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-015]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK034 Are requirements defined for existing `.envrc` files with unrelated user content? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-010, FR-011]
- [x] CHK035 Are requirements defined for existing `.envrc.local` files with unrelated user content? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-011]
- [x] CHK036 Are requirements defined for read-only or protected project environment files? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK037 Are requirements defined for unsafe owner/repository characters and normalized token storage names? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Project Repository Identity]
- [x] CHK038 Are requirements defined for a blank token input when no prior token file exists? [Edge Case, Data Model §Secret Storage Location]
- [x] CHK039 Are requirements defined for avoiding token exposure in failures as well as successful setup output? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-012, Contract §User Feedback Contract]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK040 Are assumptions clear that Codacy is project-scoped and therefore belongs in project setup rather than machine setup? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK041 Are assumptions clear that repository-token mode is recommended for one project while account-token mode is available for broader automation? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK042 Are dependencies on existing generated project agent docs documented as the place for LLM guidance? [Dependency, Spec §FR-014, Assumptions]
- [x] CHK043 Are no-new-dependency and no-lock-file constraints documented consistently with the constitution? [Dependency/Consistency, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Constitution Check]

## Notes

- Focus areas: optional submenu requirement quality, Codacy credential-mode clarity, secret-safe environment handoff, cancellation/idempotency coverage.
- Depth: standard.
- Audience/timing: PR reviewer before task generation.
